blockingIssues.description: The hosted-provider public surface is no longer feature-gated: `crates/agents-extensions/src/sandbox/__init__.rs` unconditionally defines and re-exports all hosted provider symbols, so the provider feature flags in the extensions and facade manifests no longer isolate the public export surface.

Hosted providers that support PTY currently report `supports_pty()` only when the session state has `interactive_pty = true`, so PTY capability depends on a prior request flag instead of backend support and violates the preserved-capability contract.

The rclone-backed GCS mount builder forwards HMAC and native GCS auth fields together instead of choosing one auth mode or rejecting the combination, so mixed-auth inputs do not fail clearly and do not match provider-native mapping semantics.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update `rust-sandbox-worker` to explicitly allow a compile-surface/public-export feature to establish the minimal gated API surface before finalizing a temp-crate or compile-matrix verification test when the test itself depends on the exported symbol names.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The review for `hosted-provider-feature-gates-and-public-exports` found the worker had to sketch the gated public symbol names first and only then finalize the feature-matrix verification, and the handoff recorded that as a deviation from the skill's current test-first procedure.
